# Preface {.unnumbered}

This report was completed for the final project in CP 322: Machine Learning at Wilfrid Laurier University by Romin Gandhi.

The reason why credit risk was chosen as a topic is that, being a student with a background in both computer science and finance, I wanted to work on a problem that involves both fields, and credit risk is an important issue in financial services organizations. According to data, credit card charge-offs in Q3 2024 have risen to 4.65%, the highest level since 2011 [@ncba2024chargeoffs]. It shows that there should be a better way to predict whether or not an applicant will default on their credit card balance. With this project, I hope to demonstrate an understanding of various machine learning models that will help predict whether or not an applicant will default on their credit card balance, along with the trade-offs between each of the three models used to solve the problem.
